title: ophrys transhyrcana subsp. morio
intro: Ophrys transhyrcana subsp. morio, also known as the 'Golden Bee Orchid', is a type of orchid native to the Mediterranean region. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory compounds. The plant's health benefits also include its potential to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Ophrys transhyrcana subsp. morio can be prepared as a tea or infusion, with the dried flowers and leaves being used in herbal remedies.
